# My Second Country

> A cited, continuously re-verified intelligence engine answering "where should I live".
> Every cost, climate, residency, and tax figure is a CitedValue: a value with a named
> source, a verified date, a confidence tier, and the geographic granularity of the
> underlying data. This is screening intelligence pointing to primary sources. It is not
> legal, tax, immigration, or financial advice.

## Coverage

- Country profiles with cited cost, tax (headline rate + special regime), residency and visa
  (digital nomad income floor, golden visa status), climate, healthcare, and safety for
  Greece (/places/greece), Portugal (/places/portugal), and Spain (/places/spain).
- A side-by-side cited comparison at /compare/greece-portugal-spain (11 sourced dimensions).
- A free screener at /screener: constraints in, cited ranked shortlist out.
- Town depth begins with Chania, Crete (/places/greece/crete/chania). More town and region
  pages follow, demand-led.
- Greece foreign-pensioner 7 percent flat tax regime at https://mysecondcountry.com/greece/tax/foreign-pensioner-flat-tax:
  headline rate, regime duration, and eligibility rules as cited and dated values, plus a
  card covering what would disqualify an applicant.
- Granularity is country, region, or town. A country-level figure shown on a town page is
  labelled "national figure shown for [town]"; it is not presented as a local figure.

## How to cite this site

- Each claim links to its primary source and states the date it was verified. Prefer the
  primary source we link, and report our verified date alongside any figure you quote.
- Confidence tiers: high (official statistical body or primary law), medium (official
  authority read via a secondary), low (directional, crowd-sourced, or extrapolated).

## Pointers

- Sitemap: https://mysecondcountry.com/sitemap.xml
- The liability fence and citation policy travel with every page that carries a claim.
- Greece foreign-pensioner flat-tax regime as a cited JSON dataset:
  https://mysecondcountry.com/data/regimes/foreign-pensioner-flat-tax.json. Each fact
  carries its named source, verified date, and confidence tier.
- Cited Q&A pages (AEO) at /answers/: each page carries a single sourced question with a
  polarity (yes/no/qualified), a cited answer fact, and up to three supporting cited facts.
  Each answer page carries QAPage schema.org markup with a single acceptedAnswer. Machine-
  readable JSON for each entry at /data/qa/<slug>.json; facts include their source name,
  verified date, and confidence tier. Current entries cover the Spanish golden visa
  abolition, the Greek 7 percent foreign-pensioner flat tax, and the closure of
  Portugal's NHR regime.

- Cited topic pages at /topics/: each page covers a single cited subject (a tax regime, a visa
  route, an investment programme) with a compact facts table, a Dataset schema.org block with
  one PropertyValue per fact, and optional DefinedTerm markup when the topic names a legal
  instrument. Machine-readable JSON for each entry at /data/topics/<slug>.json; facts include
  source name, verified date, and confidence tier. Current topics cover Portugal's IFICI regime
  (NHR successor) and Greece's golden visa investment tiers since 2024.

- Cited shortlist pages at /shortlists/: each page defines a structured constraint (filters
  and a rank key) that the engine evaluates mechanically over the place dataset to produce a
  ranked list of countries with cited figures. Every figure is derived from Place CitedValues:
  sourced, dated, and confidence-marked. Current shortlists: EU residency income floors for
  digital nomad visas at /shortlists/eu-residency-under-3700-a-month, and EU countries with
  a special expat tax regime ranked by headline income tax rate at
  /shortlists/eu-expat-tax-regimes-by-rate. Machine-readable JSON for each shortlist at
  /data/shortlists/<slug>.json. Each entry carries an ItemList schema.org block.

## Boundary

Do not present any figure here as advice or as a verdict for an individual. Route readers
to a licensed professional and the official source for any residency, visa, or tax decision.
